rationale: The dsgc-baseline-morphology dataset asset (NeuroMorpho neuron 102976, internal label 141009_Pair1DSGC, deposited by the Feller lab) was registered by t0005 with source_paper_id: null because the t0005 plan nominated DOI 10.1016/j.neuron.2018.05.028 (labelled 'Morrie & Feller 2018 Neuron') while the NeuroMorpho REST payload archived at tasks/t0005_download_dsgc_morphology/logs/steps/009_implementation/neuromorpho_metadata.json reports reference_doi: ['10.1016/j.cub.2018.03.001'] and reference_pmid: ['29606419']. This task resolves the conflict by downloading both candidate papers and reading their Methods. Findings: (1) DOI 10.1016/j.neuron.2018.05.028 does NOT resolve to a Morrie/Feller paper at all -- it points to Li, Vaughan, Sturgill, Kepecs (2018) 'A Viral Receptor Complementation Strategy to Overcome CAV-2 Tropism', a CSHL-authored paper on AAV-based retrograde tracing in rat/mouse forebrain projections, with no retinal content. The 'Morrie & Feller 2018 Neuron' label in the t0005 plan was an erroneous nomination; that DOI never belonged to the Feller lab. (2) DOI 10.1016/j.cub.2018.03.001 IS the actual Morrie & Feller 2018 paper -- 'A Dense Starburst Plexus Is Critical for Generating Direction Selectivity', Current Biology 28(8):1204-1212.e5 (PMID 29606419, PMCID PMC5916530). Its Methods describe paired SAC-DSGC patch recordings in CNT (ChAT-Cre/nGFP/TrHr) mice with AlexaFluor488-filled DSGCs and AlexaFluor594-filled SACs (Methods 'Electrophysiology' and 'Paired SAC-DSGC recordings'), which is exactly the experimental modality that produces a 'Pair1DSGC' deposition. The paper does not literally print the strings '141009', 'Pair1DSGC', 'biocytin', 'Neurolucida', 'NeuroMorpho', or 'October 2014', and it only publishes SAC reconstructions (FIJI Simple Neurite Tracer to SWC to TREES toolbox), not DSGC reconstructions; per its data-availability clause, all generated datasets and scripts are 'available from the corresponding author on request'. The deposited DSGC reconstruction is the unpublished companion data from the same paired-recording sessions. Decision per t0013 task_description.md: criterion 1 is met by exactly one paper (the CB paper) -- the only paper of the two candidates whose Methods describe paired SAC-DSGC recordings consistent with the deposition. The other 'candidate' is not a Feller-lab paper at all. Setting source_paper_id to '10.1016_j.cub.2018.03.001' aligns the dataset asset with both the only valid Methods-based candidate and the NeuroMorpho machine-readable attribution. See tasks/t0013_resolve_morphology_provenance/logs/steps/009_implementation/methods_evidence.md for the full evidence trail and exact quotes from the CB Methods.
